title: ranunculus flaccidus subsp. confusus
intro: Ranunculus flaccidus subsp. confusus, also known as the Western Buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It is primarily recognized for its yellow flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus flaccidus subsp. confusus include reducing inflammation, wound healing, and potential antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of this plant can be used in the form of teas, infusions, or extracts, although proper caution and guidance from a medical professional are recommended due to its potential toxicity.
